violations.text: Cinnamon Rolls with Icing packaged in the food establishment and set for customer self-selection are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. (Original Packaging with ingredient information available on site.) New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025

Several food items in the Open-Air Cooler were observed held at 42 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit in the Open-Air Cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, the food items are held in a separate refrigeration unit overnight and loaded just prior to opening. Food items were mved to colder refrigeration at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

At the time of inspection, the Open-Air Cooler was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment (Per the PIC, the unit was serviced since previous inspection). The unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 42 to 48 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures at 42 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Use of the Unit for Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods is to be discontinued until unit is fixed/adjusted and maintaining proper ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below with food temperatures maintained at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Per the PIC, Doors have been approved to order for the Open-Air Cooler. PIC to contact inspector when doors installed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/06/2024

Yogurt Parfaits, Fruit Cups, and Pudding Cups were observed held at 46 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit in the Open-Air Cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food items observed were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

At the time of inspection, the Open-Air Cooler was observed to be in need of repair/adjustment. The unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 44 degrees Fahrenheit with food temperatures at 46 to 47 degrees Fahrenheit. Use of the Unit for Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods is to be discontinued until unit is fixed/adjusted and maintaining proper ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

Sushi and yogurt was observed held at 47-49 degrees Fahrenheit in the open air cooler. Per PIC, food has been in unit for hour 4 hours. PIC voluntarily disposed of items. Ambient air temperature of unit observed to be fluctuating between 41-45 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023

The open air cooler in the front retail air observed to be in poor repair (maintaining ambient air temperature between 41-45 degrees Fahrenheit). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods to not be stored in the air cooler until unit can be replaced/repaired/serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/15/2023
